sections.paragraph:
-

(1) i.q. Arab. عوى to bend, to curve, to twist, to distort (cogn. root אָוָה), see Niph. Pi. Hiph.

(2) to act perversely, to sin (compare חָבַל No. II. 2), Dan 9:5 followed by עַל of pers. Est 1:16. (Arab. غوى to err, to be led astray.)

Niphal-

(1) to be distorted, to writhe, with pains and spasms, like a parturient woman. Isa 21:3, נַעֲוֵיתִי מִשְּׁמֹעַ “I writhe, so that I cannot hear,” also to be, bowed, to be depressed by calamities, Psa 38:7.

(2) to be perverse. Pro 12:8, נַעֲוֵה לֵב “(a man) perverse of heart.” 1Sa 20:30, בֶּן־נַעֲוַת הַמַּרְדּוּת “thou son of the perverse rebellious (woman),” i.e. of a perverse rebellious mother.

Piel, to pervert, to subvert, to overturn. Isa 24:1, עִוָּה פָנֶיהָ “he subverteth the face thereof” (of the earth). Lam 3:9, נְתִיבֹותַי עִוָּה “he has subverted my ways.” Compare הָפַךְ.

Hiphil, to make crooked, to pervert, as to pervert right, Job 33:27 to pervert one’s way, i.e. course of action, i.e. to act perversely, Jerem. 3:21 then by the omission of דֶּרֶךְ to act perversely, 2Sa 7:14 19:20 24:17 .

Derived nouns, עַוָּה, עָֹון, עִוְעִים, עִי, מְעִי Chald. עִוְיָא, and the pr.n. עַוִּי, עִוָּה, עֲוִית, עַי, עִיִּים, עִיֹּון.
